message:
So my partner rolled his eyes when I insisted we test our smoke alarms last month. "They're fine," he said. "We just changed the batteries."

Then last week, I burned toast. Not just a little char—I mean *smoke everywhere*. And guess what? Three of our four alarms went off instantly. He actually said, "Okay, okay, you were right" while waving a dish towel at the ceiling.

I didn't even gloat. (Okay, maybe a little.) But honestly? That moment made me realize why testing them actually matters—it's not about being prepared for some far-off disaster. It's about knowing your home will *actually alert you* when you need it to, whether it's burnt toast or something serious.

I found this little refresher on home fire safety that breaks down the stuff that's easy to forget about, and it genuinely made testing feel like less of a chore.

When's the last time you tested yours? 😊
